question: During a thermal runaway event in an NCM lithium-ion cell, a sequential series of internal events is recorded. At approximately 100°C, a gas analyzer detects H2 and C2H4 in the vent stream. At approximately 145°C, CO2 and O2 are detected simultaneously. At approximately 225°C, a rapid and uncontrolled temperature spike to above 900°C is recorded within seconds. A safety engineer is asked to evaluate which mechanistic interpretations of this sequence are consistent with the observed data, and whether any cross-component interactions between the stages are supported.

A prismatic cell module is being designed for two candidate chemistries: NMC-811 and LFP. During thermal runaway testing of individual cells, NMC-811 cells reach average peak temperatures of approximately 900°C and lose roughly 45% of their mass, while LFP cells reach approximately 524°C and lose roughly 21% of their mass. The NMC-811 thermal runaway reaction proceeds approximately nine times faster in energy terms than LFP. In the NMC-811 module, the time interval between successive cell thermal runaways increases exponentially, and this increase is localized entirely in the pause phase between events rather than in the internal reaction duration. In the LFP module, propagation velocity is five times lower overall. A structural engineer evaluating housing material selection and a safety engineer evaluating detection strategy must both make decisions based on these conditions. Which of the following conclusions are admissible?

A battery pack uses a composite phase change material (CPCM) layer between cells as its primary thermal protection strategy. The CPCM has a latent heat density of approximately 150 kJ/kg and a thermal conductivity of 5 W/(m·K). During a thermal runaway event in one cell, the heat release rate exceeds 800 kJ/kg. An engineer evaluates the following statements about the expected system behavior after the CPCM's latent heat is fully consumed. Which of the following statements are admissible conclusions?

An engineer is analyzing the internal-to-external temperature gradient behavior of a lithium-ion cell under two overcharge modes: constant-current (CC) and constant-current/constant-voltage (CC-CV). Under CC-CV charging, the internal-external temperature difference at 0.5 C overcharge is 55°C, while at 7 C overcharge it is 29.5°C. Under CC charging, the corresponding differences are 75.2°C at 0.5 C and 66.1°C at 7 C. Additionally, under CC charging at 5 C and 7 C, the pressure relief valve activates at approximately 988 s and 686 s respectively, accompanied by a sharp drop in internal temperature. The voltage threshold at which an abrupt voltage change occurs during CC overcharging increases with overcharge rate, from approximately 5.0 V at 0.5 C to approximately 5.26 V at 7 C. Which of the following statements are supported by this evidence?

A cell-level thermal runaway model for an NCM/electrolyte system is being validated. Calorimetry data shows that when NCM cathode material is combined with electrolyte, heat flow rises sharply starting near 250°C and peaks near 283°C, with rapid gas pressure buildup above 270°C. Separately, modeling of the same cell chemistry gives a self-accelerating decomposition temperature (SADT) near 126–139°C depending on the model used. At the anode, experimental data indicates that above 300°C the binder reaction with lithium-ions becomes the dominant exothermic process, generating H2. When electrolyte is present at the anode, s-SEI regeneration consumes available lithium-ions, which reduces the exothermic contribution from the binder reaction. A module-level propagation criterion states that if ambient temperature exceeds the SADT or surrounding cell shell temperature reaches 139°C for sufficient duration, propagation to adjacent cells will occur. Which of the following conclusions are admissible based on this information?

A kinetic model for lithium-ion cell thermal runaway is constructed by fitting parameters exclusively to DSC data. The model includes an anode reaction term representing SEI decomposition followed by remaining exothermic reactions. When the model is used in ARC cell simulations, it fails to reproduce the experimentally observed temperature gap between SEI decomposition onset and the onset of remaining exothermic reactions. A separate model calibrated directly to ARC data for the same anode reaction uses a pre-exponential factor of A = 0.035 s⁻¹ for the low-temperature range, whereas DSC-inferred pre-exponential factors for the same reaction type typically range from 10¹⁰ to 10²⁰ s⁻¹. An engineer is asked to evaluate which modeling conclusions are defensible given this situation. Which of the following statements are admissible?

A simulation study of thermal runaway propagation in a battery pack compares a full model (including both natural convection and thermal radiation) against two reduced models: one omitting natural convection and one omitting thermal radiation. The omission of natural convection produces a peak temperature that is 150 K lower and a time-to-peak that is 52 seconds earlier compared to the full model. The omission of thermal radiation produces a peak temperature that is 52.4 K lower and a time-to-peak that is 125 seconds earlier. In a separate set of simulations, the cell-to-cell gap is varied. At zero gap, natural convection does not alter the sequence in which cells enter thermal runaway. At nonzero gaps, larger gaps increase the time interval before the last cell enters thermal runaway. An engineer concludes that for pack-level thermal runaway propagation modeling, radiation is the more important heat transfer mechanism to include accurately because omitting it causes a larger time-to-peak error. Which of the following evaluations of this conclusion and the underlying evidence are correct?

An NCM 811 lithium-ion battery is stored at 50% SOC in an open environment. A safety engineer argues that this SOC represents the safest storage condition based on explosion-related metrics. A second engineer counters that storing at 50% SOC does not eliminate explosion risk and that the lower explosion limit behavior across SOC levels is non-monotonic, which complicates straightforward SOC minimization strategies. Which of the following statements are consistent with both engineers' positions and the underlying physical reasoning?

During thermal runaway of an LFP pouch cell, temperature exceeds 250°C. An analyst proposes that the H2 detected in effluent gases originates from electrolyte solvent decomposition, the same pathway responsible for early methane generation. A second analyst attributes H2 to binder-lithium reactions at the anode. Both analysts agree that CO2 and CO appear later in the gas sequence. Which assessment of these competing attributions is most consistent with the physical mechanisms involved?

An NMC-811 21700 cell undergoes thermal abuse testing at 25% SoC and again at 100% SoC in identical N2 atmospheres. A gas detection system is positioned to monitor composition from the first venting event onward. An engineer reviews the gas composition data from the safety venting phase only and concludes that H2 detection could serve as an early warning signal distinguishing high-SoC cells from low-SoC cells during this phase. Which of the following statements about this conclusion and the underlying gas behavior are supported by the evidence?

A sodium-ion battery (SIB) and an LFP battery of comparable energy capacity are subjected to identical constant-power heating in a sealed calorimetric enclosure. The SIB reaches thermal runaway at a surface temperature approximately 138°C lower than the LFP, and the interval between safety valve opening and thermal runaway onset is roughly 14 times shorter for the SIB. An engineer must assess which conclusions about relative hazard management are admissible from these conditions alone.

During thermal runaway testing of energy-matched SIB and LFP cells, the SIB produces approximately four times more gas per unit energy and has a maximum mass loss rate per unit energy roughly 6.6 times higher than the LFP. The SIB vent gas explosive limit range (ELR) is 32.5% compared to 21.21% for LFP. An engineer evaluating enclosure venting and ignition risk for a battery module must determine which conclusions are defensible.

During thermal processing of spent NMC lithium-ion batteries at 700 °C in an oxidative atmosphere for 90 minutes, carbon content is reduced from approximately 41% to under 1%. Separately, fluorine-containing electrolyte salt decomposes at temperatures that depend on whether organic solvents are present: decomposition can begin near 85 °C with solvents present, versus 150–250 °C without. After thermal treatment, fluorine is found not only in the gas phase as HF but also retained in an organic oil by-product. An engineer proposes that extending the treatment time beyond 90 minutes at 700 °C will further reduce residual fluorine in the solid product. Which of the following assessments are consistent with the evidence?

A CTP battery module undergoes thermal runaway in a closed nitrogen-filled enclosure instrumented with pressure and voltage sensors. Engineers observe that abrupt chamber pressure changes occur slightly before the recorded thermal runaway trigger times for each cell in the module. The vented gas composition is dominated by H2, CO2, and CO, with a calculated explosion limit substantially wider than methane and a lower explosive limit near 6%. Which conclusions about monitoring strategy and gas hazard are admissible given these conditions?

A cylindrical lithium-ion cell undergoes accelerating rate calorimetry. Internal and surface thermocouples are both active. The internal thermocouple records a maximum temperature of approximately 995°C and a peak rate of temperature rise near 530°C/s. The surface thermocouple simultaneously records a maximum of approximately 566°C and a peak rate near 49°C/s. Separately, the cell voltage drops sharply from above 3 V to below 0.3 V within one second, and this voltage event is recorded roughly 7 seconds before the internal thermocouple indicates the onset of the most intense thermal phase. Which conclusions are admissible given these observations?

A 2.5 Ah NMC pouch cell undergoes thermal runaway triggered by overheating. At 30% SOC, negligible gas is released and no CO2 is detected. At 100% SOC, gas volume exceeds 6 L and peak reaction temperature surpasses 529 K. An engineer argues that reducing SOC to 30% is sufficient to eliminate explosion hazard. Which statements are admissible given this evidence?

A lithium-ion cell is modeled under thermal abuse with ambient temperature held at 473.15 K after 90 minutes. The simulation shows self-heating becoming prominent at ~468.9 K (t=184 min), the self-heat rate exceeding 2°C/min at ~489.1 K (t=218 min), and peak temperature reaching 543.84 K at t=224 min. Electrolyte decomposition onset is detected at t=168 min. An engineer proposes that the onset of electrolyte decomposition is the trigger for the critical self-heat rate threshold. Which conclusions are admissible?
